title: Spicy, sweet, delicious
pedagogy: Vocabulary: เผ็ด (high ผ + dead ด → low), หวาน (ห-nam ว: high + live → rising), อร่อย (mid อ + dead → low; อ-nam ร: mid + mai ek → low — อักษรนำ like ห-nam but with อ). Negation review: ไม่เผ็ด. Register: taste words + particle keep kitchen requests friendly.

exercises.title: ไม่เผ็ด is self-defense
body: Thai kitchens assume you can take it. ไม่เผ็ดครับ/ค่ะ — “not spicy” — is the sentence that stands between you and a scorching lunch.
Fair warning: street-stall “not spicy” is still gently on fire. It's a negotiation, not a guarantee.
And when it's good, say so: อร่อย. No cook in Thailand tires of hearing it.

exercises.explanation: The unit-2 negation pattern, now saving your tastebuds: ไม่ + เผ็ด + particle.

exercises.context: The cook at a street stall asks how spicy you want it. You're a woman who wants it mild.
exercises.question: What do you say?

choices.thai: ไม่เผ็ดค่ะ

exercises.explanation: ไม่เผ็ดค่ะ — negation plus the falling statement particle. เผ็ดค่ะ orders the fire, and หวานค่ะ asks for a sweet curry.
อร่อยค่ะ — two words, instant friend of the kitchen.
